target.name: (-)-Aspergilone A
target.motivation: Aspergilone A (2) exhibits antifouling and anticancer activity. The authors aimed to develop a synthetic route with access to all its stereoisomers and to determine the absolute configuration of the naturally occurring (+)-aspergilone A.

success_criteria.free_text: Achieve >99% ee for the final product.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-C bond formation leading to the benzyl groups
key_disconnections.reasoning: To introduce the benzyl groups in the final step.
To cyclize phenol A into the isochromanone derivative.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-C bond formation at the aryl ring
key_disconnections.reasoning: To stereocontrolledly introduce the two stereocenters of phenol A.
To prepare the aryl boronic acid pinacol ester for the subsequent 1,2-boronate rearrangement.
To protect the hydroxyl groups on the phenol A precursor.
To deprotect the methoxy groups to hydroxyl groups.
To reduce the formyl group to a methyl group.
To introduce a formyl group onto the dimethoxybenzene.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The synthesis is centered around phenol A as the key intermediate. (+)-Phenol A was constructed using two sequential 1,2-boronate rearrangements with exceptional stereocontrol, allowing for the construction of all stereoisomers. The final steps involve formylation/condensation and double benzylation to complete the aspergilone A structure.

thought.gap: Need to introduce a formyl group onto the aromatic ring.
thought.decision: To introduce the formyl group, chose a modified literature protocol for the Vilsmeier-Haack formylation, expecting good yield.

outcome.observation: Formylated product 17 was obtained in 90% yield.

evidence.text_span: We started our endeavor from commercially available 1-bromo-3,5-dimethoxybenzene 16 and employed a modified literature protocol for the Vilsmeier−Haack formylation (Scheme 3).21 We obtained the formylated product 17 in 90% yield.

thought.gap: Need to reduce the formyl group to a methyl group using Wolff-Kishner reduction.
thought.decision: Attempted Wolff-Kishner reduction using toluene or PEG-400 as solvents, expecting the reaction to proceed.

outcome.observation: In the following Wolff−Kishner reduction, when employing toluene or PEG-400 as solvents, either KOH or the starting material were insoluble, respectively, and low to no conversion was observed.

evidence.text_span: In the following Wolff−Kishner reduction, when employing toluene or PEG-400 as solvents, either KOH or the starting material were insoluble, respectively, and low to no conversion was observed.

thought.background: Previous attempts at Wolff-Kishner reduction failed due to insolubility issues.
thought.gap: Need to find conditions for the Wolff-Kishner reduction that allow for good conversion.
thought.decision: To overcome insolubility and achieve reduction, first made the hydrazone by mixing hydrazine and aldehyde 17 in toluene and then added KOH and PEG-400, expecting improved solubility and conversion. Also, tried microwave irradiation to reduce reaction time.

outcome.observation: Deoxygenated 18 was obtained in 99% yield. The amount of hydrazine and the reaction time could be greatly reduced by employing μ-wave irradiation.

evidence.text_span: Eventually we first made the hydrazone by mixing hydrazine and aldehyde 17 in toluene and then added KOH and PEG-400. Deoxygenated 18 was obtained in 99% yield. The amount of hydrazine and the reaction time could be greatly reduced by employing μ-wave irradiation.23

thought.background: The goal is to synthesize phenol A. Starting from 5-bromoresorcinol would avoid the demethylation step.
thought.gap: Need to find an efficient route to the protected phenol A precursor.
thought.decision: Attempted to start the synthesis from 5-bromoresorcinol instead of 16, expecting to shorten the synthesis.

outcome.observation: Attempts to start the synthesis from 5-bromoresorcinol instead of 16, thus avoiding the demethylation step, were unsuccessful. Formylation reactions in the presence of the free hydroxyl groups were low yielding or did not proceed at all when the TIPS groups were introduced first.

evidence.text_span: Attempts to start the synthesis from 5-bromoresorcinol instead of 16, thus avoiding the demethylation step, were unsuccessful. Formylation reactions in the presence of the free hydroxyl groups were low yielding or did not proceed at all when the TIPS groups were introduced first.

thought.background: The methyl groups on 18 need to be removed to allow for protection with TIPS groups.
thought.decision: To remove the methyl groups, chose standard bis-O-demethylation with BBr3 in DCM, expecting high yield.

outcome.observation: Standard bis-O-demethylation with BBr3 in DCM proceeded in high yield.

evidence.text_span: Standard bis-O-demethylation with BBr3 in DCM and installment of the TIPS groups toward 20, using TIPS-Cl and imidazole in dry DCM, proceeded in high to excellent yields.

thought.background: The hydroxyl groups on 19 need to be protected.
thought.decision: To protect the hydroxyl groups, chose TIPS-Cl and imidazole in dry DCM, expecting excellent yield.

outcome.observation: Installment of the TIPS groups toward 20, using TIPS-Cl and imidazole in dry DCM, proceeded in excellent yield.

evidence.text_span: Standard bis-O-demethylation with BBr3 in DCM and installment of the TIPS groups toward 20, using TIPS-Cl and imidazole in dry DCM, proceeded in high to excellent yields.

thought.background: The protected aryl bromide 20 is ready for borylation.
thought.gap: Need to introduce a boronic ester group onto the aromatic ring.
thought.decision: To introduce the boronic ester, chose standard Miyaura borylation conditions with Pd(dppf)Cl2, bis(pinacolato)diboron, and potassium acetate, expecting good yield.

outcome.observation: The desired aryl boronic ester 12 was obtained in 72% yield.

evidence.text_span: Next, standard Miyaura borylation conditions of Pd(dppf)Cl2, bis(pinacolato)diboron (B2pin2) and potassium acetate were used to yield the desired aryl boronic ester 12 in 72%.24

thought.background: The aryl boronic ester 12 is ready for the first 1,2-boronate rearrangement.
thought.gap: Need to introduce the first stereocenter via 1,2-boronate rearrangement.
thought.decision: Attempted the rearrangement at ambient temperature without any additive, expecting it to proceed based on literature.

outcome.observation: While the rearrangement took place at ambient temperature without any additive, the crude reaction mixtures were more complex, possibly due to partial decomposition of the boronate complex, rendering the step not fully reproducible.

evidence.text_span: While the rearrangement took place at ambient temperature without any additive, the crude reaction mixtures were more complex, possibly due to partial decomposition of the boronate complex, rendering the step not fully reproducible.

thought.background: Previous attempts at the first 1,2-boronate rearrangement resulted in complex mixtures.
thought.gap: Need to improve the reproducibility and purity of the first 1,2-boronate rearrangement.
thought.decision: To improve reproducibility and purity, chose to add MgBr2 and perform the reaction at cryogenic temperatures, expecting to reduce side reactions and decomposition.

outcome.observation: The addition of MgBr2 resolved this problem, while reducing the reaction time as well. Suspending the crude multiple times in n-heptane facilitated the removal of the Lewis acid and no further purification was necessary.

evidence.text_span: The addition of MgBr2 resolved this problem, while reducing the reaction time as well.18 Suspending the crude multiple times in n-heptane facilitated the removal of the Lewis acid and no further purification was necessary.

thought.background: The first 1,2-boronate rearrangement product 23 is ready for the second rearrangement.
thought.gap: Need to introduce the second stereocenter via 1,2-boronate rearrangement.
thought.decision: To introduce the second ethyl group, chose to repeat the rearrangement procedure with (R)-tin benzoate 24, expecting it to proceed without additional Lewis acid as alkylboronic pinacol esters readily undergo the 1,2-rearrangement.

reaction.time: 60 min, then 3 h, then 60 min, then 4 h

outcome.observation: Repeating the rearrangement procedure with (R)-tin benzoate 24 as a reagent, the second ethyl group was incorporated, delivering compound 25. Since alkylboronic pinacol esters readily undergo the 1,2-rearrangement, no additional Lewis acid was needed for the second rearrangement.

evidence.text_span: Repeating the rearrangement procedure with (R)-tin benzoate 24 as a reagent, the second ethyl group was incorporated, delivering compound 25. Since alkylboronic pinacol esters readily undergo the 1,2-rearrangement, no additional Lewis acid was needed for the second rearrangement.20

thought.background: The alkylboronic pinacol ester 25 needs to be converted to the secondary alcohol.
thought.gap: Need to oxidize the boronic acid ester.
thought.decision: To oxidize the boronic acid ester to the secondary alcohol, chose NaBO3, expecting to yield pure O-TIPS protected (+)-phenol A 26.

outcome.observation: Oxidation of the boronic acid ester to the secondary alcohol with NaBO3 yielded pure O-TIPS protected (+)-phenol A 26 in 60% over three steps starting from 12.

evidence.text_span: Oxidation of the boronic acid ester to the secondary alcohol with NaBO3 yielded pure O-TIPS protected (+)-phenol A 26 in 60% over three steps starting from 12.

thought.background: The O-TIPS protected (+)-phenol A 26 was obtained.
thought.gap: Need to remove the silyl protecting groups. Also, considered avoiding silyl protecting groups earlier in the synthesis.
thought.decision: Attempted to avoid introducing silyl protecting groups and proceed with the aryl methyl ethers present in compound 16 to shorten the synthesis, expecting to simplify the route.

outcome.observation: We originally attempted to avoid introducing silyl protecting groups and proceed with the aryl methyl ethers present in compound 16 to shorten the synthesis, but the final removal of the methyl groups proved to be difficult (see Supporting Information).

evidence.text_span: We originally attempted to avoid introducing silyl protecting groups and proceed with the aryl methyl ethers present in compound 16 to shorten the synthesis, but the final removal of the methyl groups proved to be difficult (see Supporting Information).

thought.background: The O-TIPS protected (+)-phenol A 26 was obtained.
thought.gap: Need to remove the silyl protecting groups to obtain (+)-phenol A (5).
thought.decision: To remove the silyl groups, chose tetrabutylammonium fluoride (TBAF), expecting excellent yields.

outcome.observation: Removal of the silyl groups with tetrabutylammonium fluoride (TBAF) proceeded with excellent yields, delivering (+)-phenol A (5) in a total yield of 31% over 9 steps and with >99% ee.

evidence.text_span: Removal of the silyl groups with tetrabutylammonium fluoride (TBAF) proceeded with excellent yields, delivering (+)-phenol A (5) in a total yield of 31% over 9 steps and with >99% ee.

thought.gap: Need to cyclize (+)-phenol A (5) to the 3,4-dihydro-6H-isochromen derivative 27.
thought.decision: To obtain the desired 3,4-dihydro-6H-isochromen derivative 27, chose an excess of triethyl orthoformate and TFA, similar to conditions reported for citrinin synthesis.

outcome.observation: Compound 27 was obtained as a TFA adduct, purification of which proved to be difficult, thus the crude compound was used directly in the next step. Interestingly, (+)-phenol A (5) readily cyclized to the isochromane upon exposure to acetone, without the need of an acid catalyst, in contrast to previous reports.

evidence.text_span: To obtain the desired 3,4-dihydro-6H-isochromen derivative 27, we used an excess of triethyl orthoformate and TFA. Similar conditions had been reported for the synthesis of citrinin (1),29−31 for which additional acid was not required, presumably due to their substrate bearing a carboxylic acid moiety. Compound 27 was obtained as a TFA adduct, purification of which proved to be difficult, thus the crude compound was used directly in the next step. Interestingly, (+)-phenol A (5) readily cyclized to the isochromane upon exposure to acetone, without the need of an acid catalyst, in contrast to previous reports.29

thought.background: The 3,4-dihydro-6H-isochromen derivative 27 is ready for dialkylation.
thought.gap: Need to perform the final dialkylation to obtain aspergilone A.
thought.decision: Attempted standard alkylation conditions using BnBr with carbonate bases in polar, aprotic solvents (DMF, acetone, 1,4-dioxane, DCM), expecting good yields.

outcome.observation: Standard alkylation conditions using BnBr with carbonate bases in polar, aprotic solvents (DMF, acetone, 1,4-dioxane, DCM) led to low yields of 25% at best.

evidence.text_span: The final dialkylation was surprisingly challenging. Standard alkylation conditions using BnBr with carbonate bases in polar, aprotic solvents (DMF, acetone, 1,4-dioxane, DCM) led to low yields of 25% at best.

thought.background: Standard alkylation conditions for 27 resulted in low yields.
thought.gap: Need to improve the yield of the dialkylation.
thought.decision: Explored addition of silver salts, phase transfer catalysts, hydroxide bases, hindered amine bases, and Mitsunobu conditions, expecting to find more effective conditions.

outcome.observation: We further explored addition of silver salts, phase transfer catalysts, hydroxide bases, hindered amine bases and Mitsunobu conditions, but to no avail. Stronger bases, such as LiOH and tetrabutylammonium hydroxide (TBAOH) yielded a strong signal of the target mass in HRMS, but no target compound could be isolated. Higher temperatures and longer reaction times also seemed to be counterproductive, presumably due to the relative instability of the deprotonated 3,4-dihydro-6H-isochromen 27.

evidence.text_span: We further explored addition of silver salts, phase transfer catalysts, hydroxide bases, hindered amine bases and Mitsunobu conditions, but to no avail. Stronger bases, such as LiOH and tetrabutylammonium hydroxide (TBAOH) yielded a strong signal of the target mass in HRMS, but no target compound could be isolated. Higher temperatures and longer reaction times also seemed to be counterproductive, presumably due to the relative instability of the deprotonated 3,4-dihydro-6H-isochromen 27.

thought.background: Previous attempts at dialkylation of 27 were unsuccessful or low yielding, possibly due to instability of the deprotonated intermediate.
thought.gap: Need to find conditions for efficient dialkylation of 27.
thought.decision: To achieve acceptable yields, theorized that the substrate needed to be consumed rather quickly at moderate temperatures. Chose benzyl iodide, Cs2CO3, and 18-crown-6 in dry DMF at slightly elevated temperatures, expecting improved reactivity and stability.

outcome.observation: Eventually, using benzyl iodide, Cs2CO3 and 18-crown-6 in dry DMF at slightly elevated temperatures, (3S,4R)-aspergilone A (2) was isolated in 51% over two steps. Additionally, a minor amount of O-benzylated 27 was obtained in the last step.

evidence.text_span: Eventually, using benzyl iodide, Cs2CO3 and 18-crown-6 in dry DMF at slightly elevated temperatures, (3S,4R)-aspergilone A (2) was isolated in 51% over two steps. Additionally, a minor amount of O-benzylated 27 was obtained in the last step.

Overall Yield.interpretation: Total yield for the synthesis of (-)-ent-aspergilone A (2).

Total Steps.interpretation: Total number of steps in the synthesis.

Failed Attempts Count.interpretation: Number of explicitly mentioned failed attempts during the synthesis.

validation.comparison_to_prior: The analytical data of (3S,4R)-(2) matched reported values, except for optical rotation, where we observed the same magnitude, yet the opposite sign. This allowed for the determination of the absolute configuration of naturally occurring (+)-aspergilone A as (3R,4S).
validation.significance: First enantioselective total synthesis of (-)-ent-aspergilone A (2) with high yield and enantiomeric excess. Established the absolute configuration of naturally occurring (+)-aspergilone A (2) to be (3R,4S). The key double anionic 1,2-boronate rearrangement permits full stereocontrol and formal access to all stereoisomers, enabling synthesis of other citrinin type azaphilones.

source.title: Stereocontrolled and Adaptable Synthesis of (-)-Aspergilone A via the Key Intermediate (+)-Phenol A.
